title: paraspalathus procumbens
intro: Paraspalathus procumbens is a plant species commonly known as the "sensitive plant" due to its compound leaves that fold up when touched. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and swelling, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Paraspalathus procumbens include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, or using the leaves in a topical cream or ointment to apply directly to affected areas.
